title: diploclinium repandum
intro: Diploclinium repandum, also known as False Indian Hemp, is a plant species in the family Rubiaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a diuretic and its anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Diploclinium repandum are said to include relief from fever, headaches, and various skin conditions, as well as its potential to aid in digestion and reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
